steps.description:
This manual governs two controlled procedures on the STEMFIE construction-set vehicle: Procedure A - full assembly from the base to the completed configuration (state 13), and Procedure B - replacement of the long-brace rear chassis with the short-brace rear chassis (state 13 to state 22).
The vehicle comprises 36 printed parts organized into 11 tracked components. All verification in this manual is performed against the 11-component state model defined in step 5.
Notation used throughout: orange squares mark work instructions, red marks warnings and known error modes, blue marks informative notes, green marks verification criteria, and magenta circles mark required parts.

All fastening is performed by hand: push-fit pins and finger-tightened screws and acorn nuts. No tools are required or permitted.
Do not use pliers, drivers or other tools. Printed PLA bosses can crack under tool torque. Firm finger torque is the specified tightening level for every fastener in this manual.

Parts are color-coded for identification:
· White PLA - braces and beams (base, chassis structures, bracket)
· Silver metallic PLA - pins (4x)
· Magenta PLA - nuts, washers, wing and pulley
. Black tough PLA - wheels
Before starting, sort all parts by color and verify completeness against the chapter parts lists. Reject damaged or warped parts.

The workstation contains surplus washers, nuts, screws and pins, including several visually similar pin and washer variants. Not every part at the station belongs in the vehicle.
Verify the type and length of every fastener against the instruction for that step before installation.
Rationale: the two highest-frequency documented errors on this build are installation of a wrong pin type and substitution of an acorn nut for the bracket screw. Both are visually subtle and both have escaped automated inspection.
steps.section: STEP 4 Foreign and surplus stock control

steps.description:
Throughout this manual, checkpoints display a strip of 11 boxes. Each box represents one tracked component, always in the same fixed order: base, front chassis, front chassis pin, rear chassis, short rear chassis, front-rear pin, rear-rear pin, front bracket, bracket screw, front wheel assembly, rear wheel assembly.
An orange 1 indicates the component is correctly installed; a white 0 indicates it is not installed.
At every checkpoint, compare the build to the strip, component by component. Any mismatch is a nonconformance: stop work and proceed per chapter 8.
Example: base, front chassis and front chassis pin installed (state 5)
steps.section: STEP 5 State model and verification strips

steps.description:
The procedure is subgoal-oriented. Chapters 2 and 3 may be executed in either order, and where a step states that pin order is interchangeable, either sequence is acceptable. Twenty-two distinct correct execution orders are on record for this build.
The following are not flexible: every checkpoint must pass before work continues, and a step is complete only when the component is correctly installed and tightened. Execution of an action does not constitute completion of the step.

On completing each step, remove both hands from the assembly before verification. This provides an unobstructed view of the build state for the operator, the inspector, or a vision-based verification system.
Do not build over an unverified or suspect step. A defect concealed beneath subsequent work requires that work to be undone for rectification (chapter 8).

All 36 part geometries are open source (STEMFIE SPS-000001, scaled 200% in all axes). Fabrication parameters: 0.3 mm layer height, 15% infill, 50 mm/s, raft build-plate adhesion, 0.5 mm hole horizontal expansion.
The 0.5 mm hole horizontal expansion is mandatory; without it the pins will not achieve the specified push fit.

Select the applicable procedure from the work order:
. Procedure A - new build: proceed to chapter 2 and work through chapter 6 in chapter order (chapters 2 and 3 interchangeable).
. Procedure B - rear-chassis upgrade: proceed to chapter 7.
Procedure B requires an incoming unit verified at state 13 (cover strip). A unit not at state 13 must first be dispositioned per chapter 8.

· Base plate (1x) - white
· Front chassis brace set (1x) - white
· Front chassis pin (1x) - silver
Surplus pins are present at the station. Verify pin type and length against the work instruction before installation (step 4).
steps.section: STEP 1 Parts required for this chapter

steps.description:
Place the base on a flat surface in the standard orientation. All subsequent alignment references the base.
Inspect the part for damage or warping before proceeding.
Checkpoint 2.2: state 1 - base only

Align the front chassis braces to the front interface of the base.
Fit the chassis onto the base until fully seated: no gap at the joint, bores aligned.
A misaligned front chassis is a documented high-impact error; all subsequent steps build upon it. Verify squareness at this step.

Verify the pin: correct type, correct length, silver.
Insert the pin through the aligned bores and push to a full seat. Verify head orientation per the work instruction.
Wrong pin type and reversed pin orientation are the most frequently documented pin errors on this build and have escaped automated inspection. Manual verification is mandatory.
steps.section: STEP 4 Install the front chassis pin

steps.description:
Pin flush and fully engaged; correct type; correct orientation.
Front chassis cannot rotate or lift from the base.
Hands clear; build matches the strip below.
Checkpoint 2.5: state 5 - front chassis subgoal complete
Operator (name / signature / date)
Inspector (name / signature / date)
Result: PASS [ ] FAIL [ ]
:unselected: :unselected:

· Long braces (2x) - white
· Front-rear chassis pin (1x) - silver
· Rear-rear chassis pin (1x) - silver
Use the LONG braces. Short braces are Procedure B stock and must not be installed in a new build. If in doubt, compare brace lengths side by side.
This chapter may be executed before chapter 2; both orders are correct.
steps.section: STEP 1 Parts required for this chapter

steps.description:
Align both long braces to the rear interface of the base.
Fit until the bores align. The braces must sit parallel, bores concentric with the base bores.

Verify the pin, then insert it through the forward bore position of the rear chassis.
Push to a full seat and verify orientation.
Steps 3 and 4 are interchangeable; the two rear pins may be installed in either order.
steps.section: STEP 3 Install the front-rear chassis pin

steps.description:
Verify the pin, then insert it through the rearmost bore position.
Push to a full seat and verify orientation.
Apply the same verification as in chapter 2: wrong type and reversed orientation are the characteristic pin errors. Verify each pin as it is seated.
steps.section: STEP 4 Install the rear-rear chassis pin

steps.description:
Both pins fully seated, correct type, correct orientation.
Rear chassis secure with no play; braces parallel.
Hands clear; with chapter 2 also complete, the build matches the strip below.
Checkpoint 3.5: state 8 - both chassis subgoals complete
Operator (name / signature / date)
Inspector (name / signature / date)
Result: PASS [ ] FAIL [ ]
:unselected: :unselected:

· Front bracket (1x) - white, with magenta wing per configuration :selected:
. Front bracket screw (1x) - threaded shank with head :selected: :selected:
Acorn nuts at the station are visually similar to the bracket screw and are not interchangeable with it. This substitution is a documented error that has escaped automated inspection. Positively identify the screw before installation.
Chapters 2 and 3 must both be complete before this chapter.
steps.section: STEP 1 Parts required for this chapter

steps.description:
Align the front bracket to the front chassis interface.
Fit the bracket and hold the alignment: square to the vehicle centerline, bores aligned.

Install the verified screw by hand.
Tighten to firm finger torque: thread fully engaged, head seated flush.
HOLD POINT - before proceeding, re-confirm by head shape that the installed fastener is the screw, not an acorn nut.
Checkpoint 4.3 (hold point): state 9 - bracket and screw installed
Checkpoint 4.3 (hold point)
Operator (name / signature / date)
Inspector (name / signature / date)
Result: PASS [ ] FAIL [ ]
:unselected: :unselected:
steps.section: STEP 3 Install the front bracket screw

steps.description:
· Wheels (4x) - black :selected:
· Washers (4x) - magenta :selected:
· Acorn nuts (4x) - magenta :selected:
· Rear pulley (1x) - magenta :selected:
Count the washers before starting. Omission of wheel washers is the documented low-detectability error on this build; the assembly appears acceptable without them.
Steps 2 and 3 are interchangeable; front and rear wheel assemblies may be installed in either order.
steps.section: STEP 1 Parts required for this chapter

steps.description:
On each side of the front axle, assemble the stack in order: wheel, washer, acorn nut.
Install each acorn nut by hand and tighten to firm finger torque.
Rotation check: both wheels rotate freely with no axial play.
steps.section: STEP 2 Install the front wheel assembly

steps.description:
Install the pulley on the rear axle first.
A missing rear pulley is a documented error state with low detectability once the wheels are installed. The pulley is always installed before the wheels.
On each side, assemble wheel, washer, acorn nut, and tighten to firm finger torque.
Rotation check: both wheels rotate freely; pulley present and seated.
steps.section: STEP 3 Install the rear wheel assembly

steps.description:
A washer is present behind every wheel (4x).
All four acorn nuts tightened to firm finger torque.
Rear pulley present and seated.
Hands clear; build matches the strip below if chapter 4 is complete.
Checkpoint 5.4: state 13 - all components installed
Operator (name / signature / date)
Inspector (name / signature / date)
Result: PASS [ ] FAIL [ ]
:unselected: :unselected:

Hands clear. Verify the complete vehicle, component by component, against the strip below.
Every component at 1 except the short rear chassis, which remains at 0 in the new-build configuration.
Acceptance criterion: state 13 - complete assembly

Front bracket secured by the screw (not a nut), fully tightened.
All four pins: correct type, correct orientation, fully seated.
All four acorn nuts tight; washer behind every wheel; rear pulley present; wing present per configuration.
All four wheels rotate freely; no component exhibits play.

Record the build: executed step order, checkpoint results, and the final state vector. On PASS, release the vehicle.
On any FAIL, do not release. Quarantine the unit and proceed per chapter 8; every component has a sanctioned removal and rework path.
Final inspection - Procedure A
Operator (name / signature / date)
Inspector (name / signature / date)
Result: PASS [ ] FAIL [ ]
:unselected: :unselected:
